key_insight: A symmetric dual-chip piezo strain-cell design can deliver homogeneous uniaxial strain to thick, square quantum-device substrates while minimizing bending and shear.
engineering_relevance: The platform enables cryogenic strain tuning of semiconductor quantum devices with practical RF/DC wiring density and electrical isolation from high-voltage actuators.
method: Dual-chip loading geometry to symmetrize the applied load
Finite element simulations to assess strain homogeneity and deformation modes
Grounded Faraday-cage enclosure plus high-density RF/DC interposer for device integration
evidence_signal: Experiments on a 200 µm-thick silicon die showed 215 µε surface strain at 200 V piezo bias, consistent with the simulation-supported design.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful mechanical symmetry and isolation; wiring or mounting asymmetries could reintroduce flexure, shear, or parasitic electric-field effects.
paper_title: A Cryogenic Uniaxial Strain Cell for Quantum Devices
